conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately, overall?
I’m fine, just a bit tired. I work a lot, so I don't get as much rest as I should.
How has being tired affected the things you usually enjoy?
Not too much, really. I still watch TV after dinner, but I might doze off a bit faster than usual.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah, definitely. I get a bit distracted easier now. I just try to take breaks and get back to it.
How long do those breaks usually need to be before you can refocus?
Just a few minutes. I can usually get back to it pretty quickly.
How have you been sleeping lately?
It's been okay. I wake up a bit in the night sometimes, but it doesn't bother me too much.
How many times would you say you wake up on a typical night?
Maybe once or twice? It's nothing too crazy.
Been doing anything you enjoy lately?
Yeah, I went to a concert last weekend. It was a lot of fun.
What kind of music was it?
Just a local band. They were pretty good, though!
When you think about the next few weeks, what do you expect day to day?
Just the usual stuff. Work, work, work. Maybe I'll take a weekend trip or something, but nothing too big.
Does the idea of that weekend trip feel like something you're genuinely looking forward to, or more like something you feel you should do?
A bit of both, probably. I like the idea of it, but I'm also just trying to get out of the house for a bit.
Do you find it harder to make decisions than before, even small ones?
Maybe a bit. I overthink things sometimes. But I just try to tell myself it doesn't matter that much and move on.
When you overthink, what kind of things tend to get stuck in your mind?
Just little things like what to eat for lunch. I'll look at a menu for 10 minutes deciding.
Do you feel like you have enough energy for your usual day?
Yeah, I do. I just might be a bit more tired than usual.
Can you tell me a bit more about what "a bit more tired than usual" feels like for you?
Just a bit more heavy, I guess. I still get things done, but I might not be as bouncy as usual.
Are you still into the things you used to care about?
Yeah, totally. I just might not have as much time for them right now.
What's one thing you wish you had more time for right now?
Just to relax more, I think. I'm always doing something.
Is there anything weighing on your mind that you wish you could change?
Just work stuff. It's a bit stressful, but it's not anything I can really change.
What about the work stress makes it feel unchangeable to you?
Just the fact that it's my job, I guess. I have to do it.
How do you feel about how things have gone for you recently?
It's been okay. Just a bit of a slog, but it's normal.
What makes it feel like a slog lately?
Just the daily grind, I guess. It's the same thing every day.
How's your appetite been?
It's been good. I eat fine.
Have you noticed any changes in what you're craving lately?
Not really. Just the usual stuff.
Have you found yourself getting emotional more easily lately?
Maybe a bit. I get frustrated with traffic sometimes.
What else tends to bring out that frustration for you?
Just small things like when my internet is slow. It's just annoying.
Do you feel physically restless or on edge in your body?
Not really. I just get a bit snappish sometimes.
When you feel snappish, do you notice any tension in your body?
Maybe a bit in my shoulders. I just roll them back and it goes away.
Is it worse when you have to sit still or wait?
Yeah, definitely. When I'm waiting in line, I get a bit fidgety.
Does that fidgeting feel like a physical restlessness you can't control?
Kind of. I just pace back and forth until I can leave.
When you need to choose, do you get stuck or go back and forth?
Yeah, a bit. I'll scroll through my phone for 10 minutes deciding what to watch.
Does that kind of hesitation happen when you're making bigger decisions, too?
No, not usually. Just little things.
Does even a simple choice feel like too much sometimes?
Maybe a bit. I'll look at a menu and be like, 'Ugh, I don't know what to eat.'
Does that feeling of not knowing what to eat happen with other everyday choices, too?
Not usually. It's just food.
Is it more 'heavy and slow' or 'drained after little effort'?
More heavy and slow, I think. I just feel like I'm moving through molasses.
How long has it felt like moving through molasses?
Just a few weeks, I guess. It's nothing new.
Does rest actually help, or are you still flat afterward?
It usually helps a bit. I just feel a bit more tired than usual, but I can still do stuff.
How long does that bit of extra tiredness usually last?
Just a few hours. I'm fine by evening.
Is the problem falling asleep, staying asleep, waking too early, or sleeping too much?
Just waking up a bit in the night. It doesn't keep me up long, though.
What part of that has been the hardest for you day to day?
Just getting out of bed. It takes a bit longer than usual.
How many nights a week is it like that?
Maybe three or four.
Can you tell me more about what those nights are like for you?
It's just annoying, I guess. I'll just get up and do something quiet until I can fall back asleep.
